description: Dynamic RADOS Cluster Registry + Zero-Downtime Add. Closes ROADMAP P2 'Dynamic RADOS / S3 cluster registry + zero-downtime add.' Today the gateway loads the RADOS cluster set ONCE from `STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS` env at startup via `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::buildDataBackend` â†’ `datarados.ParseClusters` â†’ `Backend.clusters` map. Adding a new RADOS cluster requires a full gateway restart. US-044 of a prior cycle shipped the multi-cluster connection map (`b.connFor`, `b.ioctx`) but not its lifecycle. Fix scope: (1) persist cluster catalogue in `meta.Store` (new `ClusterRegistry` CRUD across memory + Cassandra + TiKV); (2) admin API `GET/POST/DELETE /admin/v1/storage/clusters[/{id}]` wired into `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml`; (3) `rados.Backend` watcher goroutine polls meta every `STRATA_CLUSTER_REGISTRY_INTERVAL` (default 30s), set-diff reconciles `Backend.clusters` map â€” lazy-dial on add, safe-drain on remove; (4) FULLY RETIRE the `STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS` env path â€” registry is the SINGLE source of truth, no two-source confusion. S3-backend half deferred (single-bucket-per-instance today; per-instance lifecycle is a separate P2 followup). Operator workflow: bootstrap = POST first cluster via admin API after gateway starts (admin API works without RADOS). No migration tooling needed â€” no existing deployments to migrate. 7 stories. Closes ROADMAP P2 'Dynamic RADOS / S3 cluster registry + zero-downtime add.' on cycle close.

userStories.title: meta.Store ClusterRegistry CRUD + memory backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `meta.Store` to expose `ListClusters / GetCluster / PutCluster / DeleteCluster` on the memory backend so the admin API + watcher have a persistent catalogue source.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `ClusterRegistryEntry` struct to `internal/meta/store.go`: fields `ID string`, `Backend string` ("rados" / "s3"), `Spec []byte` (opaque JSON-encoded config), `CreatedAt time.Time`, `UpdatedAt time.Time`, `Version int64` (monotonic CAS-on-update counter)

Add to `meta.Store` interface: `ListClusters(ctx) ([]*ClusterRegistryEntry, error)`, `GetCluster(ctx, id string) (*ClusterRegistryEntry, error)`, `PutCluster(ctx, e *ClusterRegistryEntry) error` (insert or CAS-update on Version), `DeleteCluster(ctx, id string) error`
Sentinel errors: `meta.ErrClusterNotFound`, `meta.ErrClusterVersionMismatch`
Implement on `internal/meta/memory/store.go` under existing per-store mutex; `PutCluster` validates Version-on-update == stored.Version, bumps Version, stamps UpdatedAt

Add `caseClusterRegistry` to `internal/meta/storetest/contract.go`: insert two clusters â†’ List returns both sorted by ID â†’ Get returns one â†’ Put with stale Version returns `ErrClusterVersionMismatch` â†’ Delete + Get returns `ErrClusterNotFound` â†’ List-empty returns nil slice (NOT error)
Memory backend test (`internal/meta/memory/store_test.go`) wires `caseClusterRegistry`
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Cassandra backend ClusterRegistry CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Cassandra meta backend to persist the cluster registry so a multi-replica gateway sees the same catalogue.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `cluster_registry` table to `internal/meta/cassandra/schema.go::tableDDL`: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS cluster_registry (id text PRIMARY KEY, backend text, spec blob, created_at timestamp, updated_at timestamp, version bigint)`. Idempotent

Implement `ListClusters / GetCluster / PutCluster / DeleteCluster` on `internal/meta/cassandra/store.go`. `PutCluster` uses LWT `IF NOT EXISTS` for insert and `IF version = ?` for update â€” LWT-on-LWT pattern per CLAUDE.md gotcha
`PutCluster` on stale Version returns `meta.ErrClusterVersionMismatch`
Cassandra integration test (`internal/meta/cassandra/store_integration_test.go`, build tag `integration`) runs `caseClusterRegistry` against testcontainers Cassandra
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (memory contract still green; `make test-integration` green)

userStories.title: TiKV backend ClusterRegistry CRUD
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the TiKV meta backend to persist the cluster registry so the TiKV-backed gateway has parity.
acceptanceCriteria: Add cluster_registry key encoding to `internal/meta/tikv/keys.go`: prefix `clusters/<id>` (variable-length string segment via FoundationDB-style byte-stuffing per `keys.md`). Schema-additive

Implement `ListClusters / GetCluster / PutCluster / DeleteCluster` on `internal/meta/tikv/`. `PutCluster` uses pessimistic txn (`Begin â†’ LockKeys â†’ Get â†’ CAS-on-Version â†’ Set â†’ Commit`) â€” same shape as `SetBucketVersioning` per CLAUDE.md gotcha
Early-return CAS-reject path calls `txn.Rollback()` explicitly â€” no LockKeys lease leak per CLAUDE.md gotcha
TiKV contract test passes (`caseClusterRegistry` runs against all 3 backends)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Admin API GET/POST/DELETE /admin/v1/storage/clusters
userStories.description: As an operator, I want HTTP endpoints to list, register, and de-register cluster catalogue entries so I can add a new RADOS cluster without restarting the gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `internal/adminapi/storage_clusters.go` with three handlers wired into the router: `handleListClusters` (GET /admin/v1/storage/clusters), `handleCreateCluster` (POST /admin/v1/storage/clusters), `handleDeleteCluster` (DELETE /admin/v1/storage/clusters/{id})
POST request body shape: `{id, backend, spec}` JSON. Validates `backend âˆˆ {rados, s3}`, `id` matches `[a-z0-9-]{1,64}`, `spec` is well-formed JSON and non-empty. Format-only â€” no dial-test
POST conflict (id already exists) â†’ 409 `ClusterAlreadyExists` (new APIError sentinel)
DELETE on missing â†’ 404 `NoSuchCluster`

DELETE on cluster referenced by a `ClassSpec.Cluster` in the running config â†’ 409 `ClusterReferenced` with response body `{referenced_by: ["STANDARD", "COLD"]}` (the storage class names that map to the cluster). Use in-memory `Backend.classes` map snapshot â€” no manifest scan
GET list returns `[{id, backend, created_at, updated_at, spec}]` sorted by id

Audit-log integration: handlers call `s3api.SetAuditOverride(ctx, action, resource, bucket, principal)` with `action="admin:CreateCluster"|"DeleteCluster"|"ListClusters"`, `resource="cluster:<id>"`, `bucket="-"` per CLAUDE.md admin audit pattern
OpenAPI contract `internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml` updated with the three endpoints

Unit tests `internal/adminapi/storage_clusters_test.go`: happy path (POST â†’ GET â†’ DELETE â†’ GET-after-delete = 404); validation (malformed JSON â†’ 400, unknown backend â†’ 400, id pattern â†’ 400); conflicts (POST-twice â†’ 409, DELETE on referenced â†’ 409 with `referenced_by`)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: RADOS Backend registry watcher + hot-reload
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the running `rados.Backend` to pick up cluster catalogue changes within one poll interval â€” lazy-dial on add, safe-drain on remove â€” without restarting the gateway.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `internal/data/rados/registry_watcher.go`: `RegistryWatcher` struct wraps `meta.Store` reference; `Start(ctx)` spawns a polling goroutine (every replica polls â€” diff is idempotent, no leader needed). Poll cadence via `STRATA_CLUSTER_REGISTRY_INTERVAL` (default 30s, clamped [5s, 5m]) read once at watcher construction

On each tick: compute set-diff against `Backend.clusters` map under `b.mu`. Added â†’ merge spec, `connFor` lazy-dials on next traffic. Removed â†’ close cached conn + ioctxes under lock, remove from map. Updated (same id, bumped Version) â†’ replace + re-dial on next traffic
Watcher honours `ctx.Done` â€” exits cleanly on `Backend.Close`
Add `Close(ctx) error` to `data.Backend` interface (idempotent; memory backend Close is noop; rados backend Close drains pool + stops watcher; s3 backend Close drops the client)

Initial sync: `rados.New(cfg)` fetches the registry once synchronously via `meta.ListClusters`, populates `Backend.clusters` from it. If registry empty, `Backend.clusters` is empty; subsequent RADOS traffic fails fast with `unknown cluster` until operator POSTs at least one entry. Gateway starts cleanly + admin API works
Wire `cfg.Meta` in `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::buildDataBackend` after `buildMetaStore` runs

Unit test `internal/data/rados/registry_watcher_test.go`: fake `meta.Store` returns 3 clusters â†’ tick â†’ `backend.clusters` has 3 entries. Fake returns 2 (one removed) â†’ tick â†’ 2 entries, removed-cluster's cached conn closed. Fake returns 4 (one added) â†’ tick â†’ 4 entries. Race-detector clean
Goroutine leak test: `Backend.Close(ctx)` cancels the watcher; assert no leak via `runtime.NumGoroutine()` baseline + delta within tolerance
Prometheus counter `strata_cluster_registry_changes_total{op="add"|"remove"|"update"}` exported on the default registry â€” incremented on each watcher reconciliation
Reference doc `docs/site/content/reference/_index.md` gains `STRATA_CLUSTER_REGISTRY_INTERVAL` entry
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Retire STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS env path
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the legacy env-based cluster config fully removed so there is exactly ONE source of truth â€” the registry.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove `Clusters` field from `internal/config/config.go` `Config.RADOS` struct AND the `"STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS": "rados.clusters"` entry from the koanf env-map
Remove `cfg.RADOS.Clusters` argument from `datarados.New(...)` call in `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go::buildDataBackend`

Remove `ParseClusters` / `BuildClusters` / `ValidateClusterRefs` from `internal/data/rados/clusters.go` if they no longer have callers; keep `ClusterSpec` struct (still used by the registry + ioctx path)
Update existing tests + examples that lean on `STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS` to pre-seed the in-memory ClusterRegistry via `meta.Store.PutCluster` directly

Update `deploy/docker/docker-compose.yml` + `deploy/docker/docker-compose.ci.yml` to drop `STRATA_RADOS_CLUSTERS` from the strata service env; document new bootstrap path (POST via admin API after gateway start)
Update `scripts/s3-tests/run.sh` + `scripts/s3-tests/README.md` if they reference the env var; switch to admin API POST in the smoke harness
`make smoke` still passes â€” smoke harness uses admin API POST in startup hook to register the test cluster
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
